name: EasyEcom-Item-Pull
change_reason: Stage 2 reconciliation against real captured GetProductMaster payloads (tests/ee_mock/getproductmaster_response.json, getproductmaster_child_product.json). Replaces the stale Bidirectional EasyEcom-Item-Sync ruleset which had: wrong source paths (base_uom -> EE actually sends accounting_unit; weight_grams -> EE actually sends weight); missing fields (cost, mrp, size, colour, dimensions, brand, active, ee_product_id, ee_cp_id); no transform for the EE active->ERPNext disabled flip; a placeholder compose ref to EasyEcom-Item-UOM-Sync which is empty and not used in Stage 2. The matching push ruleset (EasyEcom-Item-Push) is a Stage 3 build.

rules.notes: EE's sku is the unique identifier per its FAQ; we use it as item_code on create. Stage 2 §8.1.3 matches sku==item_code byte-for-byte.

HSN gating handled by the FLOW (item_pull.py). The ruleset passes the value through; the flow checks whether the HSN exists in tabGST HSN Code and Flag-Not-Creates the product when absent (India Compliance makes HSN-less Item un-createable; we do not placeholder per §8.1.4). We avoid validate_against on the rule because it would raise mid-rule and prevent the flow from reading the other fields it needs for the FNC map row's diagnostic context.

EE's accounting_unit is dirty in real payloads (mob000=111, Top3=333, Nike=11, SKINQ=PCS, child product NA). The FLOW checks whether the produced value matches a real UOM doc; if not, substitutes the Account's default_uom and flags the Item as Created-Flagged. The substitution doesn't happen in the ruleset because the validity check requires a DB lookup against tabUOM and the flag-or-not decision is flow-level.

Flips polarity: EE active=1 (alive) maps to ERPNext disabled=0; EE active=0 (dead) maps to disabled=1. Uses conditional_constant rather than enum_map because EE's int 1 wouldn't match a JSON map key (always str), and to handle both '1'/'0' string and 1/0 int defensively.
EE weight is in grams; ERPNext weight_per_unit takes a Float; weight_uom set as a custom-field convention since we don't write into Item.weight_uom on pull.
EE cost stored on Item for visibility; NOT written into Item.valuation_rate (auto-managed by ERPNext stock ledger).
EE MRP = ERPNext default selling rate (best-fit).
Preserve the original EE MRP independent of standard_rate (which the FDE may later override).

name: EasyEcom-Item-Push
change_reason: §8d Stage 3 — ERPNext Item → EasyEcom CreateMasterProduct / UpdateMasterProduct payload. Holds the REAL-DATA-FROM-ITEM mappings only (Sku, ItemName, ProductTaxCode, dimensions, Cost, productId for updates). The MANUFACTURED EE-mandatory fields ERPNext doesn't carry (materialType=1 Finished Good, itemType=0 normal in Stage 3, Brand/Category/ModelNumber fallbacks, TaxRate snapped to EE's allowed {0,3,5,12,18,28} set) are injected by the FLOW (item_push.py) after the engine produces this payload — they're flow decisions, not translations, and don't belong in the ruleset per the §8.0 engine-as-translator policy. Stage 3 covers normal products only; bundles (combo_product) are Stage 4's job and use itemType=1 + subProducts. | v2 (FDE-configurability refactor per user direction): the manufactured constants (materialType=1, itemType=0, Brand fallback, Cost fallback chain, ModelNumber=item_code) are now ruleset rules instead of flow-code constants. FDE edits them via the desk EasyEcom Field Mapping > EasyEcom-Item-Push > Rules child table — no code deploy needed to change defaults or add company-conditional logic. TaxRate stays in the flow (it's a DB lookup + snap-to-set, not a constant or simple derivation). | v3 (§8d Stage 4 — bundles): itemType becomes per-record conditional (Product Bundle wrapper → 1, normal → 0) by reading source_doc.flags.is_bundle_wrapper which the FLOW sets before push. Wrapper-Item push (combo path) and normal-item push share the same ruleset; the flow's pre-push flag-set decides which itemType the ruleset emits.

rules.notes: EE's Sku is its primary key for products (per its FAQ). ERPNext's item_code is the byte-for-byte source — the §8d pull also uses sku==item_code for auto-mapping.

EE's Create/UpdateMasterProduct field for the product display name is `ModelName` per their API contract (table column 'ModelName: String, Enter the Model Name of the Product'). Pre-fix this rule sent `ItemName` which is NOT in EE's contract - EE silently ignored it and returned 200 'Updated Successfully' without applying the change. Confirmed against the Harmony sandbox 2026-05-26 - name updates only propagated after switching to ModelName.
Item.gst_hsn_code is required on Item save (India Compliance), so a successfully-saved Item always has it. The flow doesn't need to re-check; an item without HSN would never reach push.

EE requires Brand on Create. If Item.brand is set (Link to Brand DocType), push its name; otherwise fall back to 'Unbranded'. FDE: edit transform_args.expression in this rule's row to change the fallback string (e.g. 'House Brand') or add company-conditional logic. The pull direction is identity — EE-side brand strings land on Item.brand and a Brand row gets auto-created later by a Stage-5 cleanup if needed.
ERPNext Item.item_group is required, so this always has a value. Pushed as Category — EE will create/lookup its own side category from the string.

EE contract: Weight is Integer (grams). ERPNext Item.weight_per_unit is a Float with a sibling Item.weight_uom Link. The expression below reads weight_uom and converts to grams, then int-rounds for EE. FDE: edit transform_args.expression to add UOMs your client uses (e.g. "Pound": 453.592). If weight_uom is empty or unrecognised, value is treated as grams (back-compat with EE-pulled items where weight already arrived in grams). 0g still flags as Not-Pushed by the push validator - that's intentional.

EE contract: Length is Integer (cm). Item.ecs_length_cm is a read-only Float populated by the pull (already in cm). The expression below reads an optional source_doc.ecs_dim_uom and converts to cm; if no such field exists or it's empty, value is treated as cm (default). FDE: to enable non-cm input, add an 'ecs_dim_uom' custom field on Item (Data or Link to UOM) - the rule already reads it. Multiplier table is shared by Length/Height/Width.
EE contract: Height is Integer (cm). Same UOM-aware conversion as Length/Width (see Length rule's notes for the FDE extension story).
EE contract: Width is Integer (cm). Same UOM-aware conversion as Length/Height (see Length rule's notes for the FDE extension story).

EE contract: Double (mandatory on Create). Prefer EE-origin cost captured at pull (ecs_ee_cost). Fall back to Item.valuation_rate when the item was ERPNext-originated (never pulled). Final fallback to Item.standard_rate (the FDE-entered selling rate, a reasonable approximation when no costing has happened yet). Last fallback: 0 (EE accepts). FDE: edit transform_args.expression to change the chain - e.g. add 'last_purchase_rate' or reorder.

transform_args.expression: value if value else (source_doc.valuation_rate or source_doc.standard_rate or 0)

rules.notes: EE contract: Mrp is Double (optional). ERPNext's Item.standard_rate is the FDE-entered selling-rate-list price - a reasonable MRP source if no separate MRP field is maintained. Push direction: identity (float to Double). Pull direction: noop - EE's Mrp doesn't overwrite ERPNext's standard_rate (FDE may have ERPNext-side pricing logic that's authoritative). FDE: change erpnext_path to a different field (e.g. a custom 'ecs_mrp' Float field) if MRP is tracked separately from selling rate.

EE's UpdateMasterProduct expects productId as INTEGER (`{"productId": 61160356}` per the EE-provided contract). We store it as string in Frappe so int_to_str on pull / str_to_int on push. Without the int cast EE rejects the call with body code 400 "product/sku doest not exist" - the type comparison silently fails. Only meaningful on Update; on Create the field is absent on the Item until EE returns product_id and the flow writes it back.

PULL only. EE's UpdateMasterProduct/CreateMasterProduct contract does NOT accept cpId as a writable field (the per-channel-partner identifier captured on pull but never echoed back on master writes). The VALUE EE stores as cp_id on read IS what EE's update contract expects in the `productId` field (EE's read/write naming is inconsistent) - that translation happens in flows/item_push._push_update, not here. transform_push='noop' makes the executor skip this rule on push so cpId is never sent.

ERPNext Item has no native model_no field; we use item_code as ModelNumber for EE (uniquely identifying, always present). FDE: change transform_push to 'custom_python' with a different expression if your client maintains a separate model-number custom field (e.g. "source_doc.ecs_model_no or source_doc.item_code").

EE-mandatory on Create; absent from ERPNext Item. Default 1 (Finished Good) — per §8.1.5 / packet's stated default. The rule's erpnext_path is item_code as a trigger source (the transform ignores the value and returns the constant); EE-side is materialType.
FDE: edit transform_args.default (or add a condition) to change the value — e.g. raw materials = 2, or company-specific via 'source_doc.item_group in ("Raw", ...)' → 2. No code deploy needed.

EE itemType: Child=0 (normal), Combo=1, Kit=2.
Per-record conditional: the FLOW sets source_doc.flags.is_bundle_wrapper = True before calling executor.push() when the source item is a Product Bundle wrapper (frappe.db.exists('Product Bundle', {'new_item_code': item_code})). The sandbox forbids direct frappe.db lookups in expressions, so the lookup happens in the flow; the ruleset reads the pre-set flag via source_doc.flags (a frappe._dict — returns None for missing keys, no AttributeError when the flow hasn't set it — Stage 3 normal path therefore defaults cleanly to 0).
FDE: if you ship a Kit type later, add a second condition {when: source_doc.flags.is_kit, then: 2} above the bundle one — flow code would similarly set the flag.

name: EasyEcom-Customer-Sync
change_reason: RETIRED in §8e Stage 3 (2026-05-26). This ruleset mapped a dead EE shape (customer_id / customer_name / mobile / gstin / customer_category) from an older EE API. The real /Wholesale/v2/UserManagement endpoint we integrate against in 8e uses c_id / companyname / gstNum / billing+dispatch addresses — see EasyEcom-Customer-Pull (added in this commit) for the live shape. Left in the fixture as active=0 so the historical reference is preserved; the engine ignores inactive rulesets per the DocType's own description. Safe to delete the JSON block entirely if the FDE wants the cleanup; doing so on a separate commit so the deprecation event has its own SHA.

name: EasyEcom-Customer-Pull
change_reason: §8e Stage 3 — reconciled against the captured Harmony /Wholesale/v2/UserManagement?type=b2b payload (process/ee_mock_fixtures/getcustomers_b2b_response.json, 23 real records). The engine translates EE fields into a FLAT dict; the flow (customer_pull.py) splits into Customer + 2 Address docs and handles GSTIN gating, pincode-state validation, and the customer_type=Company convention. Replaces the now-retired EasyEcom-Customer-Sync which mapped a dead EE shape. Anonymous marketplace buyers (§11/§12) use EasyEcom-Customer-Anon-Pull — out of 8e scope.

rules.notes: EE's c_id is the read-side identifier (Int in EE; stored as Data on the Customer Map ee_c_id field). The push uses customerId (= c_id value) — stored separately on the Map. cpId-equivalent for §8e.

EE's `companyname` is the wholesale partner name — maps to ERPNext Customer.customer_name. Dirty duplicates exist in real data (e.g. 'TEST CUSTOMER' appears twice across c_ids); the FLOW does NOT auto-match on this field — pull always creates a new Customer when there's no map row. 'Never wrongly link > never duplicate' (§8e packet decision #1).

EE's gstNum is the GSTIN (or empty / 'URP' for unregistered parties). India Compliance enforces 15-char format on Customer.validate and throws on invalid. The FLOW catches that throw and lands the row as Flagged-Not-Created (no placeholder; the FDE fixes the source). URP handling: empty/URP → gst_category='Unregistered' + empty gstin (the FLOW sets gst_category; the engine just passes the value).
EE's customer_support_email maps to Customer.email_id. May be empty in real data — Permissive missing_field_policy lets the rule skip silently.
EE's customer_support_contact maps to Customer.mobile_no. May be empty string in real data.
EE's currency_code (e.g. 'INR') maps to Customer.default_currency. ERPNext expects a 3-letter ISO 4217 code matching a Currency record.
Address fields — flat-namespaced; the flow splits these into a Billing Address doc linked to the Customer via Frappe's standard Address.links Dynamic Link.
EE returns state NAME (e.g. 'Karnataka'). On push, EE's CreateCustomer needs the int stateId for billingStateId; Stage 4 will resolve via the Stage 2 cache. Stage 3 pull just stores the name.

name: EasyEcom-Customer-Push
change_reason: §8e Stage 4 — ERPNext Customer + linked Addresses → EE /Wholesale/CreateCustomer & /Wholesale/UpdateCustomer payload. SEPARATE from EasyEcom-Customer-Pull per §8.0 engine policy (one ruleset per direction). The engine produces a flat dict with EE keys; the FLOW (customer_push.py) injects the manufactured/per-op fields: random password on Create, state name→id resolution for Create's billingStateId/dispatchStateId (Update uses name; the flow handles the create-vs-update split at the wire boundary). The customerId field is keyed by the Customer Map's ee_customer_id on Update; on Create it's returned by EE and written back to the map row.

rules.notes: EE's create mandatory `companyName`. ERPNext Customer.customer_name is required so this always has a value. Note the camelCase — EE Create uses `companyName` while Read returns `companyname` (lowercase). This direction-specific difference is why we use a SEPARATE push ruleset rather than reusing pull's `companyname` rule. Not marked required: at the ruleset level — the FLOW does the required-presence check so missing input produces a clean Flagged-Not-Created Map row instead of an engine exception.

EE's create mandatory `email`. The flow checks presence before building the payload; missing email → flag-not-pushed (Customer Map row, no EE call). Update uses the same field name + same value. Same flow-vs-ruleset division as companyName above.
Optional on both Create and Update.

EE's `taxIdentificationNumber` field. When ERPNext gstin is empty AND gst_category='Unregistered', the FLOW substitutes 'URP' (EE's sentinel for unregistered parties) rather than sending empty. Required on Create; optional on Update (we still send it on update so EE keeps GST current).
Required on Create; ERPNext Customer.default_currency. Fallback to 'INR' handled by the flow if empty (Indian wholesale onboarding default).

Address fields are FLOW-sourced from the Customer's Address records (Frappe Dynamic Link). The flow reads Billing/Shipping Address.address_line1 and writes here; the ruleset just declares the EE field name + types it as identity-pass-through.
EE wants billingPostalCode as Integer on Create. ERPNext Address.pincode is Data — str_to_int casts at the boundary. Missing/non-numeric → flow flags.

**Update path uses this NAME.** Create path needs `billingStateId` (int) instead — the FLOW (customer_push.py) does the create-vs-update split at the wire boundary, resolving name→id via Stage 2 state_resolver when building the Create payload, leaving the name in place for Update.
EE's Create field name is `country` (just the name string, no id). Fallback to 'India' handled by the flow.
Update path uses NAME; Create path uses `dispatchStateId` (int) — same flow-level split as billingState.

name: EasyEcom-Customer-Anon-Pull
change_reason: Initial library fixture (§5.11) — marketplace anonymous-customer pattern

name: EasyEcom-Supplier-Sync
change_reason: RETIRED in §8f Stage 3 (2026-05-27). This ruleset mapped a dead EE shape (name ↔ vendor_id) that conflated the §8.3 two-identifier split — EE's read key is vendor_c_id (the join key on getVendors, e.g. 99358), and vendor_id (= vendor_code, e.g. 'VN00001') is the WRITE key returned by CreateVendor. Using either as the ERPNext Supplier.name is wrong on both counts: vendor_c_id is just an int with no narrative value, and vendor_id is account-mutable. The real /wms/V2/getVendors endpoint we integrate against in 8f uses vendor_c_id / vendor_code / vendor_name / address.{billing,dispatch}.* with a nested-or-empty-array address envelope — see EasyEcom-Supplier-Pull (added in this commit) for the live shape. Left in the fixture as active=0 so the historical reference is preserved; the engine ignores inactive rulesets per the DocType's own description. Mirrors the §8e Stage 3 retirement pattern (EasyEcom-Customer-Sync). PO-Push / GRN-Pull still reference vendor_id directly — to be repointed to the EasyEcom Supplier Map.ee_vendor_id lookup in §9/§10 (NOT this stage).

name: EasyEcom-Supplier-Pull
change_reason: §8f Stage 3 — reconciled against the captured Harmony /wms/V2/getVendors payload (process/ee_mock_fixtures/getvendors_pages_response.json, 30 real records across 2 cursor pages). The engine produces a FLAT dict; the flow (supplier_pull.py) splits into Supplier + 2 Address docs and handles GSTIN+PAN gating, country-aware (Indian vs Foreign → Overseas), lifecycle (active:0→disabled), the address-may-be-empty-array envelope, and the supplier_type=Company convention. TWO-IDENTIFIER SPLIT: vendor_c_id is the READ key (the unique join key on the Supplier Map), vendor_code is the WRITE key (= vendor_id used by CreateVendor/UpdateVendor). BOTH are captured on pull so the §9/§10 PO/GRN flows can resolve via the Supplier Map without re-reading. Replaces the now-retired EasyEcom-Supplier-Sync (active=0) which mapped a dead shape. The FLOW pre-flattens the nested address.{billing,dispatch}.* envelope before invoking the engine — EE returns `address: {billing: [], dispatch: {...}}` mixed dict/empty-array shapes, and the engine reads flat keys (mirrors EasyEcom-Customer-Pull which uses billingStreet etc.).

rules.notes: EE's vendor_c_id is the READ-side identifier (Int in EE; stored as Data on the Supplier Map ee_vendor_c_id field). It's the unique join key (autoname format:ECS-SUPP-{ee_vendor_c_id}). NOT used by push — see ee_vendor_id below.

EE's vendor_code is the WRITE-side identifier (also called vendor_id; e.g. 'VN00001'). CreateVendor returns it as data.vendor_id (echoes vendor_code); UpdateVendor consumes it as `vendorId`. Captured separately from vendor_c_id on every pull so the §9/§10 PO/GRN flows can resolve ERPNext Supplier → EE write key via the Supplier Map without re-reading EE.

EE's `vendor_name` is the supplier company name — maps to ERPNext Supplier.supplier_name. The flow does NOT auto-match on this field (same dirty-data reasoning as customer pull — EE re-uses names across vendor_c_ids); pull always creates a new Supplier when there's no map row. 'Never wrongly link > never duplicate' (§8f packet).

EE's tax_identification_number is the GSTIN for Indian suppliers (or blank for foreign / unregistered). India Compliance enforces 15-char format on Supplier.validate AND auto-extracts PAN from gstin[2:12]. The FLOW catches IC's throw on bad-format (or country=India + blank GSTIN if the supplier truly is registered) and lands the row as Flagged-Not-Created. For FOREIGN suppliers (resolved via country lookup), the flow sets gst_category='Overseas' BEFORE IC validate so GSTIN is optional.

EE's pan is the PAN field (10 chars, often dirty in real data — 9-char or null observed). The flow only needs PAN separately when GSTIN is blank (Indian unregistered + foreign); when GSTIN is valid, IC auto-extracts PAN from gstin[2:12] and the value here is informational only.

EE's contact_number is a free-text field; may be empty in real data (Incirco Test Supplier shows ''). Permissive policy lets it skip silently when blank. ERPNext Supplier.mobile_no is the standard field; ERPNext fetches it from the linked Contact / Address but the direct Supplier.mobile_no field is read-only — stored separately as a Contact in Stage 3.
EE's email is the primary supplier contact email. ERPNext Supplier.email_id is read-only (fetched from Contact); stored as a Contact in Stage 3 alongside the Supplier.
EE's currency_code is the 3-letter currency code (INR for Indian, USD/EUR/etc. for foreign). The flow falls back to the Account's company's default_currency if blank.

EE's `active` is the supplier lifecycle flag (0/1). Vendors HAVE this (unlike customers in §8e). The flow maps active:0 → Supplier.disabled=1 (and active:1 → .disabled=0) so EE-side deactivation propagates to ERPNext on pull.

Pre-flattened key from address.billing.address (the flow flattens before invoking the engine — EE's nested envelope may be `address.billing: []` empty-array, which dotted paths handle poorly). When billing is empty-array, this key is absent and Permissive policy lets the rule skip silently — the flow treats absence as no-address (no row inserted).

Country name as it appears in EE (e.g. 'India', 'Italy', 'Armenia'). The flow resolves via Stage 2's resolve_country to determine Indian vs Foreign — drives the GST gating (Indian → IC GSTIN+PAN validation; Foreign → gst_category='Overseas').
Pre-flattened from address.dispatch.address. Empty-array `dispatch: []` — 16 of 30 sample vendors — means no dispatch address; flow skips Address creation rather than crashing.

name: EasyEcom-Supplier-Push
change_reason: §8f Stage 4 — ERPNext Supplier + linked Addresses → EE /wms/CreateVendor & /wms/UpdateVendor. SEPARATE from EasyEcom-Supplier-Pull per §8.0 engine policy (one ruleset per direction). LIVE FINDINGS (Harmony 2026-05-27): (1) The mandatory tax field on CreateVendor is named `taxIdentificationNum` — EE docs / packet said `taxIdentificationNumber` but the live error '_taxIdentificationNum is a mandatory Field!_' disclosed the actual name. (2) CreateVendor response carries BOTH ids in data: `data.vendor_id` (echoes the input vendor_code — the WRITE key) AND `data.vendor_c_id` (the NEWLY-assigned READ key). Both are written back to the Supplier Map after a successful create. (3) UpdateVendor request body keys `vendorId` to the WRITE key (vendor_code string); UpdateVendor response returns `data.vendorId` as the READ key (vendor_c_id int) — confirms the §8.3 packet's 58614 hypothesis. (4) State everywhere on push as NAME (no name→id resolution needed, unlike §8e customer push). NO password (vendors aren't portal logins).

rules.notes: EE's create mandatory companyName. Not marked required: at the ruleset level — the FLOW does the required-presence check so missing input produces Flagged-Not-Created instead of an engine exception.

EE's CreateVendor `vendorCode` is the WRITE key seed. ERPNext doesn't have a native vendor code field — the FLOW manufactures one from Supplier.name (sanitised to ASCII-safe characters; max length per EE constraint). Optional on EE; if omitted, EE auto-generates a code.

EE's create mandatory emailId. Sourced from Supplier.email_id which fetches from the linked Contact — the FLOW reads this via _gather_supplier_payload_dict (Supplier has no direct email field, only a fetch_from on the Contact link).
Optional on EE per the docs, but a §8e finding showed EE rejected CreateCustomer when missing — keep the rule and let the FLOW omit when blank.
Optional. EE shows it as a separate field on getVendors; the FLOW splits supplier_name on first whitespace as a fallback when no Contact link exists.

EE's create mandatory tax field. FIELD NAME IS taxIdentificationNum (live finding — NOT taxIdentificationNumber as the EE docs / §8f packet said). The FLOW substitutes 'URP' for Indian Unregistered suppliers; foreign suppliers (gst_category=Overseas) can pass an empty string (the FLOW handles the country-aware gating).
EE's create mandatory PAN (capital). The FLOW auto-extracts from gstin[2:12] when blank but GSTIN is present (mirrors IC's behaviour); foreign suppliers can pass empty.
EE's create mandatory 3-letter currency code. FLOW falls back to 'INR' when Supplier.default_currency is blank.

EE's CreateVendor has a SINGLE-address payload (street/city/state/zip/country at top level), unlike the dual billing+dispatch envelope on getVendors. The FLOW prefers the Supplier's Billing Address row; falls back to Shipping when Billing absent. **FIELD NAME IS `street` (LIVE FINDING 2026-05-27)** — the EE docs / earlier §8f packet used `address`, but live probe of 8 candidate names showed only `street` persists round-trip through `getVendors.address.billing.address`; `address` and 6 other variants were silently dropped by EE (HTTP 200 but read-back empty). **ASYMMETRY:** UpdateVendor cannot change the street value at all — every candidate field name (flat AND nested) returns 'Updated Successfully' but no-ops EE-side. Subsequent ERPNext-side street edits will surface as drift on the next pull (correct behaviour); resolving such drifts requires manual EE-side correction or vendor recreation, since the API offers no street-update path. Documented in BUILD_TRACKER §8f standing items.
EE accepts zip as string; no name→int resolution like state. Keep as identity.

STATE AS NAME on push — both Create and Update use the name (no name→id resolution like §8e customer push). This is a §8.3 specific simplification; the EE backend resolves the name internally for vendors.
Lead-time field. ERPNext Supplier doesn't have prep_days natively — Stage 4 leaves it as a parked custom-field hook (FLOW omits when missing). EE accepts it on both create + update.
Lead-time field. Same parked-custom-field caveat as prep_days.

name: EasyEcom-Location-Pull
change_reason: §8a refactor — renamed from EasyEcom-Warehouse-Pull. Reconciled to real /getAllLocation payload (the assumed paths location_id/name/pincode/is_active were stale; real payload uses location_key/location_name/zip/stockHandle plus a nested 'address type' object). is_wms_location is derived from stockHandle as a ruleset transform (conditional_constant) so an EE rename can be fixed by an FDE in the desk, not a code deploy. api_token is deliberately NOT mapped — it is a credential-shaped string per §7.7 and is redacted at the API Call log layer via utils/redaction.REDACTED_FIELDS. userId and the literal-space-key 'phone number' are also not mapped.

rules.notes: is_wms_location is derived from EE's stockHandle (1 → WMS plan, 0 → OMS-only). Expressed as a ruleset transform — an FDE can adjust the condition (e.g. if EE renames the field or adds new truthy values) without a code deploy. The FDE may still override the resulting value on the form.

name: EasyEcom-Tax-Category-Sync
change_reason: Initial library fixture (§5.11)

name: EasyEcom-Channel-Pull
change_reason: §8b refactor — reconciled to real /current-channel-status payload. Removed the channel_type rule (no such field in real payload — FDE-classified via the Marketplace Classification Workflow, gated on the Classify transition). Replaced the stale is_active rule (was bool_to_yn against assumed 'is_active' field) with a conditional_constant transform that maps real payload field 'status' (string 'Active'/'Inactive') to Check (1/0). The per-location sweep (flows/channel_discovery.py) calls this ruleset's pull() per element of data[] and then unions/dedupes by marketplace_id.

rules.notes: EE sends marketplace_id as Int (2, 60, 122). Marketplace.marketplace_id is Int (§31.2.18). identity — no coercion needed.

Real EE payload field is 'status' (string 'Active' / 'Inactive'). Marketplace.is_active is a Check (1/0). The conditional_constant transform expresses 'Active → 1, else 0' AS A RULESET RULE — an FDE can adjust the condition (e.g. if EE renames the status field or adds new active-equivalent values) without a code deploy. This is the §8.0 engine-as-API-insurance contract.

name: EasyEcom-PO-Push
change_reason: §9 Stage 1 repoint (2026-05-28). Was: supplier ↔ vendor_id direct, treating supplier.name as the EE write key. Now: supplier resolves via EasyEcom Supplier Map.erpnext_name → ee_vendor_id (the §8.3 two-id model's WRITE key, e.g. 'VN00001'). Uses the new lookup_field transformer (filter_field=erpnext_name, target_field=ee_vendor_id). Stage 2 wires the missing-Map-row fallback to flag-not-pushed; Stage 1 just repoints. Also renamed `po_reference` → `referenceCode` to match the live /WMS/Cart/CreatePurchaseOrder shape (per §9 packet contract section). `vendor_id` → `vendorId` (camelCase, matches EE wire shape). transaction_date → expDeliveryDate via date_format (EE field name from packet; was po_date which doesn't exist on CreatePurchaseOrder). The line-item compose ruleset name stays EasyEcom-PO-Line-Push (built in Stage 2).

name: EasyEcom-GRN-Pull
change_reason: §9 Stage 1 repoint (2026-05-28). Was: supplier ↔ vendor_id direct, treating EE's vendor_id as supplier.name. Now: supplier resolves via EasyEcom Supplier Map.ee_vendor_c_id (READ key, int) → erpnext_name. DIFFERENT field from PO push (which targets ee_vendor_id, the WRITE key) — the §8.3 two-id model pays off here: pull keys on read, push keys on write, both stored on the same Supplier Map row. Field names corrected to the live /Grn/V2/getGrnDetails shape: vendor_c_id (not vendor_id), grn_invoice_date (not grn_date), po_ref_num (not po_reference). The line-item compose ruleset name stays EasyEcom-GRN-Line-Pull (built in Stage 3).
